professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we are going to talk about whether it has become easier to maintain good health than it was in the past. As the economy prospers and technologies evolve, more and more people have come to realize the importance of maintaining good health. Though people nowadays know and care more about health, the problem is, is it more challenging or easier for people to maintain good health today?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think it has become easier for modern people to keep healthy. Thanks to economic growth, people have enjoyed higher incomes and thus higher living standards. Therefore, most people, compared to people in the past, have more money at their disposal to spend on healthier lifestyles. In other words, they can afford proper medical treatments while they are sick. Besides, they can eat in a more nutritious and balanced way.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Although I share Claire’s view that people have become wealthier, I still believe that it has become more challenging for people to maintain good health. Due to technological progress, life has become more convenient: people can do almost anything indoors with just a few taps or clicks. They can just order food and shop online, take online courses, and entertain themselves without heading out to do everything in person, unlike people in the past. As a result, people today exercise much less than ever before, and such laziness, inevitably, has considerably contributed to many more health problems today.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Humans live in the environment, depending on it as well. We need everything from the environment: food, clothing, medicine, and the list goes on. As the economy develops, we humans are struggling to strike a balance between the economy and the environment. Before the next class, I want you to talk about the following question: Should we slow down economic development to protect the environment?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think economic development comes before the environment. If people cannot live well enough, they would ensure their survival even at the cost of the environment. Especially in less developed areas, when the slow economic growth can hardly meet people's basic needs, the environment would be at stake. To be more specific, people need food and products to support themselves and their families. When they are short of proper supplies of living essentials, it is understandable that they would grow food and manufacture products to survive, even at the cost of the environment. They are not the ones to blame because survival is the priority of everybody. In this case, the culprit of the worsening environment is the backward economy in such underdeveloped areas.

students.name: Paul
students.text: I still think that the top priority is the environment. Without the environment and everything it has been and will be providing for humans, we cannot even exist, not to mention boosting the economy! For the long-term development and prosperity of human society, we should realize the significance of environmental protection and slow down the economy to better preserve animals, plants, and our planet Earth. Only by protecting the environment can humans and human civilization go a long way.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: As environmental protection develops, more people have come to realize that everybody should play a part in preserving the planet we live on. These years, many people want to protect and improve the natural environment. In terms of what individuals in their daily lives could do if they want to protect the environment, some put forward walking or bicycling instead of driving a car to work or school, while others recommend buying locally grown organic foods grown without pesticides, and which one do you think would work better?

students.name: Lila
students.text: I think walking or cycling instead of driving works better in reducing carbon emissions. Everybody needs to commute, and a lot of people drive every day. If they can switch to walking or riding bicycles, the outcome of such a collective effort would be considerable.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Buying locally grown organic foods is more effective, I think. Growing crops organically means giving up pesticides to get rid of pests, which significantly reduces pollutants that pose great threats to soil and water. Therefore, the food chain could be less contaminated.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: As we’ve previously discussed, sorting and recycling home waste can significantly help reduce pollution and preserve our environment. Though it has become a standard practice in some countries, many people believe that we haven’t done enough, and the only way to increase recycling is for governments to enforce strict policies on recycling. Say, if you don’t recycle your trash, you may get fined, or even break the law. What’s your perspective on this?

students.name: Mia
students.text: I do think strict policies or legal requirement is the only way to raise the recycling rate. Making laws work well in many fields, because people tend to avoid legal consequences or financial punishment. If there is no strict regulation, most people won’t take it seriously.

students.name: Joshua
students.text: Admittedly, government intervention is an important factor in this issue. However, it’s not necessarily the only means out there. I think that education can promote recycling in a more fundamental way because people would do it if they know they are doing the right thing.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Mental health has been a major focus in the field of well-being. Understandably, people who live in competitive situations, like college students, are particularly more prone to stress, so today let’s talk about stress management. Staying alone or spending time with others, which do you think is better to relax?

students.name: Maria
students.text: I would say that staying alone is more helpful in terms of reducing stress. I believe many people would agree that social activities are actually an important source of stress. Imagine you have to hang out with your friends. You’ll have to take into account your friends’ preferences and make compromises every now and then. If you relax just by yourself, you can decide what you do and what snack you eat, which to me is truly relaxing.

students.name: Carl
students.text: It is true that social activities can sometimes seem stressful, but I think that kind of stress is far less serious than the “real stress”, say when you are facing a major test or you don’t have enough money to pay rent. In such cases, people might not be able to relax if they stay alone because they cannot help thinking about the stress. That’s when friends are needed to distract a little bit.

professor.name: Professor Clarke
professor.text: As discussed in class, the concept of health has evolved over time, and with advancements in technology and medicine, it is often believed that staying healthy is easier in modern times. However, some argue that current lifestyles and environmental factors have made it more challenging to maintain good health. In which period would you argue it is easier for people to stay in a healthy condition—at present or in the past? Why?

students.name: Amber
students.text: I would definitely say that it is easier to maintain good health nowadays than in the past. It is important to consider the various factors that contribute to people's health. In the past, access to healthcare was limited and medical knowledge was not as advanced as it is today. Also, people's lifestyles were often less healthy due to factors such as limited access to healthy food and a lack of awareness about the importance of exercise.

students.name: Jonah
students.text: I hold a different view from Amber and believe that it was actually easier to stay healthy in the past. Most people led healthier, more active lives in the past so there was no need to go the gym in their spare time. There were far fewer sedentary jobs in those days so there was no obsession to keep fit. Also, in terms of the issue of food and diet, there were far fewer additives and processed foods in the past, meaning that people had healthier diets.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we're going to talk about environment protection. Airplanes release a lot of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases into the atmosphere, polluting the air and contributing to climate change. To address this problem, some countries have introduced an additional tax on air travel called an ecological tax——an extra charge added to the price of an airplane ticket. Money collected from this ecological tax will be invested in less polluting modes of transportation, such as trains, which passengers are encouraged to use instead of airplanes. Do you think there should be an ecological tax on air travel?

students.name: Olivia
students.text: There should be an ecological tax on air travel. Air travel contributes significantly to climate change, and the environmental damage it causes is not reflected in the cost of airplane tickets. By introducing an ecological tax, the true cost of air travel would be reflected in the price of tickets, and passengers would consider alternative modes of transportation. Additionally, investing the money collected from the tax in less polluting modes of transportation, such as trains, could lead to a more sustainable future.

students.name: Michael
students.text: Although I share Olivia’s view that an ecological tax on air travel helps to conserve the environment, I believe that taxing air travel would be harmful to the economy. In my opinion, air travel is a luxury and those who can afford it should pay for the environmental damage it causes. Additionally, the revenue generated from the tax could be used to invest in alternative modes of transportation that could boost the economy. Therefore, I suggest that an ecological tax on air travel is unnecessary

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we're going to talk about environment protection. The world is facing numerous environmental problems, such as pollution and global warming, which have been causing severe impacts on human health and ecosystems. There are growing concern and efforts made by many people in various countries. I’d like you to talk about this question: The environmental situation around the world is not likely to improve in the future.

students.name: Claire
students.text: Unfortunately, the environmental situation may not improve considering the current situations. Many governments prioritize economic development over environmental protection and do not implement strict regulations and policies to address environmental issues. In addition, the demand for energy, food, and water is growing rapidly, leading to higher levels of pollution and resource depletion.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Samantha’s view is reasonable to some extent, but I believe that there are positive trends and actions that could lead to improvements in the environmental situation. For example, many countries have adopted renewable energy sources and implemented sustainable practices in agriculture and forestry. Moreover, public awareness and education have increased, leading to changes in behavior and lifestyle that contribute to environmental protection.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we're going to talk about environment protection. As environmental concerns become increasingly urgent, many people want to protect and improve the natural environment. What individuals should do in their daily lives if they want to help the environment?

students.name: Claire
students.text: Recycling and reusing objects instead of throwing them in the trash is a vital step individuals can take to help the environment. By recycling materials like paper, plastic, and glass, individuals can reduce the amount of waste sent to landfills, which not only saves space but also conserves resources. Additionally, reusing objects such as containers and bags reduces the need for single-use products, which contribute significantly to waste and pollution.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Buying locally grown, organic foods is a crucial step individuals can take to help the environment. This action reduces the carbon footprint associated with transportation and support sustainable farming practices. Additionally, this also reduces the amount of harmful chemicals and pesticides that are used in conventional farming, which can have detrimental effects on the environment and human health. Organic farming practices also promote soil health and biodiversity, which can have positive impacts on local ecosystems.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we're going to talk about environment protection. National governments can take a variety of actions to help protect the natural environment, since protecting the environment is essential for promoting sustainable development and ensuring the well-being of both present and future generations. So what do you think the most important for a nation's government to take to protect the natural environment and why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: The government should pass and enforce laws to prevent pollution since it has the potential to make the biggest impact on reducing environmental degradation and safeguarding public health. Big companies and industries are often major polluters which change the health of people and wildlife alike. By passing and enforcing strict laws that regulate and limit these activities, governments can reduce the amount of pollution, and prevent many of the negative consequences.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Even though laws sometime work but I think the government preserve natural places such as forests and protect native wildlife species that live there. Forests are home to a wide variety of plant and animal species. Moreover, forests benefit human societies, including sources of food, medicine, and recreation.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we're going to talk about saving land. The earth is now witnessing a controversial issue in the using and saving of land. If no more land is available for people to explore, the living space will no longer endure the pressure from the growth in population. On the other hand, if the land that animals inhabit is invaded by humans, an increasing number will reach the verge of extinction. So what do you think is the most important，use land for human needs or save land for endangered animals?

students.name: Claire
students.text: It is more important to use land for human needs. Firstly, humans are the most intelligent species on Earth, and therefore, we have the ability to make rational decisions about the use of land. Secondly, the population of the world is increasing rapidly, and this means that more land is needed to accommodate our growing needs.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Saving land for endangered animals is crucial for the long-term health and well-being of our planet. Endangered animals play a vital role in maintaining the natural balance of ecosystems and preserving biodiversity. Moreover, the loss of one species can trigger a chain reaction that affects many other species, leading to a domino effect that can disrupt entire ecosystems.

professor.name: Professor Davis
professor.text: As we all know, the education system plays a crucial role in shaping the future of any society by providing a strong foundation for the upcoming generations. However, there are different ways of instruction for high school teachers across the nation and this has led to a debate on their merits. Which approach would you argue is better—teacher-centered education or student-centered education? Why?

students.name: Alisa
students.text: I strongly believe that teacher-centered education is the better approach for high school instruction. In this type of education, the teacher is the expert and the students are there to learn from them. This approach ensures that students receive a comprehensive and structured education that covers all necessary topics. It may seem like a good idea that student-centered education places the focus on the student's interests and preferences, but it often results in a lack of structure and a failure to cover important topics.

students.name: Jason
students.text: Unlike Alisa, I strongly believe that student-centered education is the better approach. I mean, this method prioritizes the needs and interests of the students, allowing them to take an active role in their own learning. It is more likely for them to retain information and develop critical thinking skills. Also, such an education promotes collaboration and communication among peers, preparing students for the real world where teamwork is essential. Therefore, I think the student-centered way of instruction is better.

professor.name: Professor Kind
professor.text: As discussed in class, knowing history is essential for understanding the present and shaping the future. It provides us with a sense of identity and helps us to understand the complexities of the world we live in. It also helps us to develop critical thinking skills, as we learn to analyze and interpret historical events and their impact on society. Before next class, I would like you to discuss this question: Should studying history be made compulsory for all university students, regardless of their main field of study? Why?

students.name: Amy
students.text: I would definitely say that all university students regardless of their major should be required to study history. History teaches us about the successes and failures of past societies, and the lessons we can learn from them. Additionally, studying history promotes empathy and understanding of different cultures and perspectives. By making history a compulsory subject, universities can ensure that all students have a well-rounded education and become informed and engaged citizens.

students.name: Jeremy
students.text: I hold a different view from Amy and believe that not every college student should learn history. While history provides valuable insights into the past, it may not be relevant or necessary for students pursuing certain fields of study. For example, a student studying engineering may not benefit from studying ancient civilizations. Also, forcing students to study history may lead to disinterest and lack of engagement, ultimately defeating the purpose of education.

professor.name: Professor Smith
professor.text: As I mentioned in class, field trips have been a staple of primary education for decades. They offer students the opportunity to learn outside of the classroom and engage with the world around them. Field trips can take many forms, from visits to museums and art galleries to trips to zoos and historical sites. While some argue that field trips provide hands-on learning experiences that cannot be replicated in the classroom, others believe that students can learn just as effectively by staying in the classroom What is your opinion? Why? Before next class, I would like you to share your idea on the discussion board.

students.name: Anita
students.text: I am of the opinion that it is important to take students on field trips rather than stay in class the whole time. The most important point relates to the need for variety in children’s learning experiences. School can become a little repetitive for children and anything that provides novelty and a change from the daily routine is beneficial for the students. Field trips can present the opportunity for an adventure and new experiences.

students.name: Justin
students.text: I hold a different view from Anita and firmly believe that field trips are not necessary for effective learning. In fact, students can learn just as effectively by staying in the classroom, using abundant high-quality online resources, including videos, audios and images. This means that modern technology can provide stimulating and varied learning opportunities without leaving the classroom. Also, field trips can be expensive and time-consuming, which can be a burden for schools and families.

professor.name: Professor Harris
professor.text: As we all know, video games have become an increasingly popular form of entertainment for people of all ages. However, their use in educational settings has been a topic of debate for many years. In recent times, the idea of incorporating video games into the classroom for young children aged 5-10 has gained traction. While some educators believe that video games can be an effective tool for learning, others argue that they are a mere distraction with no real benefit for education. What is your opinion? Why? Before next class, I would like you to share your idea on the discussion board.

students.name: Alice
students.text: I would definitely say that video games can be used to good effect in the classroom at this age. Children love variety and, therefore, teachers should use many different resources in the classroom to help learning. Video games can be a very effective way to grab attention with simulating images and sounds, and getting attention is particularly important with primary age children. Another important consideration is the real-life application of some of the skills practiced when playing video games, such as hand-eye coordination, reactions, and problem solving.

students.name: Joe
students.text: I hold a different view from Alice and firmly believe that video games should not be encouraged as learning tools at primary schools. My initial point refers to the addictive nature of video games for young people. They can dominate attention at the expense of everything else. For this reason, it is problematic to attempt to use them as a classroom learning tool. Another reason concerns the effect on behaviour of some games on children. There is some evidence that violent video games can make some children more aggressive.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, let's talk about an eternal topic: parent-child relationship. Traditionally, and now still the case in many regions of the world, parents emphasize discipline and even authority when interacting with their children. More and more parents, however, consider their children equal to themselves and make friends with them. Which type of relationship is more conducive to a healthy development of children?

students.name: Claire
students.text: Parents should of course make friends with their kids. That way, children can enjoy a casual atmosphere at home. At school, children are normally stressed out by their school work. When they come home, they deserve a cozy environment instead of "behaving themselves" all the time. Also, if parents treat children as friends, children are more likely to share their thoughts and struggles with their parents, so their parents can provide timely and effective suggestions and help when necessary.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Although I enjoy a good relationship with my parents, I still possess my deepest reverence for them instead of treating them merely as "friends". I think parents should still maintain discipline while getting along well with their children. Discipline is necessary, especially when children are still young, since they lack the concepts of right and wrong. It is their parents' duty to teach what to do and what not to do. Plus, if parents do not discipline their children, they will have a hard time fitting in the world in their adulthood. After all, there are so many rules for them to follow outside their sweet home.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: When people are asked about the decisive factor in determining the lasting time of friendship, they usually mention something very obvious, like being friendly or kind. But there are thousands of other elements that have a huge impact on how long we maintain our relationship with friends. What factors—other than friendliness and kindness—would you choose as being important in your long-term relationship with your friends? Why?

students.name: Wendy
students.text: Common interests with friends help us to stay connected over a long time. With common interests, we could have so many shared experiences. For example, if you both love cooking, you could try out a new recipe together. Or every time an episode of your favorite show comes out, you could talk on the phone. We all have moments like this and this shared interest is like an anchor that generates exclusivity between you and your friends, which could cement or solidify the friendship.

students.name: Jacobs
students.text: I think long-term friendship requires a reciprocal commitment. That means the exchange of something between people. For example, helping each other during crisis is a decisive factor to deepen our intimacy with friends. When our friends are having a rough week, a phone call, a patient listening, or a care package of snacks to them would help turn a whole week.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Nowadays we talk a lot about how to build relationships with others. And of course, we all want to find ways to establish meaningful and enriching bond with others. Before next class, I would like for you to discuss this question: Is being completely honest with friends helps to solidify the friendship? Or is it considered as being naive and not flexible?

students.name: Helen
students.text: Being brutally honest with friends is the secret to maintaining intimacy with our friends. When we are utterly frank with our friends, we are building mutual trust. I read that a relatively good number of relationships fall apart due to numerous lies, whereas pure sincerity with friends usually guarantees a solid friendship.

students.name: Tim
students.text: Pure honesty is not practical. We probably have encountered situations where we dislike something about our friends intensely. Invariably, we tell our friends how wonderful everything is, to avoid hurting their feelings. Or we compliment our friends for how well they look or express our appreciation for gifts, when we don’t really mean it. I think these harmless and well-intended white lies are necessary social lubricant.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: The number of overweight children in many places has doubled in recent years. Causes of obesity in children include unhealthy food choices, lack of physical activity and family eating habits. This rise in the number of overweight children is disturbing, because it causes health problems and can lead to social problems. Overweight children are more likely to be teased by their peers or to develop low self-esteem or body image problems. What do you think of this phenomenon? Do you think the negligence of parents is the main cause of childhood obesity?

students.name: Zoe
students.text: For me, there are other reasons that contribute to the obesity rate in children. School-age children spend most of their time at school without their parents around. They eat three meals a day at school, so the school should be primarily responsible. I heard that some schools collaborate with McDonald's to provide children with lunches such as hamburgers and fried chicken. Therefore, children have developed the habit of eating foods containing high calorie since childhood, which is likely to leads to weight issues.

students.name: Kelly
students.text: Well, parents should take primary responsibility for their children's physical problems. A lot of children develop obesity because their parents don’t know how to choose or prepare healthy foods. There are also parents buying whatever their kids like. Thus, children eat fattening foods on a daily basis and develop unhealthy eating habits from a young age.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: For better or worse, celebrities have a powerful impact on how teens view themselves and how they see the world. In fact, it’s easy to underestimate how much celebrities influence teenage attitudes and behavior. Celebrities can have a positive influence on youth. In fact, they can serve as role models. But famous people, whether they’re singers, actors, and other celebrities, can also provide unhealthy examples. Do you think celebrities have a positive or negative impact on young people?

students.name: Eric
students.text: The benefits celebrities bring to young people should never be underestimated. Many celebrities are open about their struggles with the world and mental health. As a result, they help to reduce stigma and raise awareness. Their previous experience, such as overcoming depression, getting rid of drug abuse, inspires young people nationwide to open up about their own hardships and provides an opportunity to address the underlying issues and build better coping strategies.

students.name: Emily
students.text: I think celebrity images have a profound negative impact on teen body image. Many celebrities, such as singers and movie stars, not only have professional strengths, but also have outstanding appearances. They share their photos on social media, young people, especially girls compare themselves to images they see of celebrities. Among them, almost half said that celebrity images make them feel dissatisfied with the way they look according to a survey.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Since the mid-twentieth century, major developed countries have experienced quickly dropping fertility rates. In recent decades, such a problem has spread throughout the world. China, the country with the biggest population, as yet, experienced its first population shrinkage since last century in 2022. Many fear that ever dropping fertility rates could even lead to the disappearances of some countries or ethnic groups. Nations are thus trying all kinds of measures to restore fertility rates, including longer maternity leaves, affordable to free childcare, monetary stimulation like tax refunds, and teaching men to get more involved in child-raising, etc. Which measure, from this list or otherwise, do you think could be effective in encouraging people to have children?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think longer maternity leaves should be its top option if a country is to stimulate its citizens to have children. It goes without saying that women make the greatest sacrifice throughout the process of raising children. Pregnant women have to endure enormous inconveniences and sicknesses caused by body swelling; the level of pain of labor is unimaginable to men; and when kids are really young, new moms have to breast-feed kids till the age of one to two. Physical discomfort, chaotic schedules, and forced detachment from regular social circles, when combined, impose huge stress on women. When facing such challenges, women would need some structured social support to overcome the fear of having babies. Maternity leave is the most practical social support a society could offer. Don't get me wrong, I am not saying that maternity leaves are enough to offset women's sacrifices in childbirth, but what else can a society offer?

students.name: Paul
students.text: I am afraid there is little to do to reverse decreasing fertility rates. Claire is absolutely right in pointing out the suffering and sacrifice of women in childbirth, but are men exempted from any inconvenience or sacrifice? After becoming a father, a man also has to share the chore of taking care of his baby, soothe his wife's suffering and mental stress, give up a large part of his sleep, and work harder to make ends meet. So, childbirth is exhausting for husband and wife alike. In the modern society, most couples could work hard and live on their savings and pensions in their later years rather than rely on their children, once the biggest motivation to have children. When people lack a strong motivation to have children, none of these measures could really work.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Over the next few weeks, we’re going to look at lots of different materials about the role of parenting in people’s lives. But first, I want to know what you think about this topic. So here’s a question for the class discussion board: What do you think is the most significant lesson that parents can teach their children? Why do you think this lesson is important?

students.name: Diane
students.text: I think the most important thing that parents can teach their children is the importance of sleep. Nowadays sleep deprivation is not a rare occurrence, but sleep is crucial for every aspect of our health. And when we don’t have enough sleep, there are consequences. Just think about how irritable we would be with insufficient sleep. Even worse, we get disease or sickness.

students.name: Matt
students.text: I am thinking about certain attitude towards life. To be specific, I think positivity is probably the most important thing parents can teach their children. Like, for example, many people work hard but they complain a lot. Never before has our lives been so stressful. I am thinking of hard-working gritty people who have no joy in their lives.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: We are all under great influence of our parents, especially on the major we choose in college. And of course, parents all have good intentions to get involved in our decision-making process. Before next class, I would like for you to discuss this question: Should parents try to help determine the futures of their children? Or children should be allowed to make their own choices?

students.name: Dalio
students.text: It’s better to let children make their own decisions. Not just because if parents are too protective, they may raise old infant who are incapable of making any important decisions in their life. But also, to build self-esteem or to become confident, children need to learn to make their own judgment. It’s our life and we should be responsible for it.

students.name: Phil
students.text: Parents are just helping children to learn about the hard truth in society. They are sophisticated adults and have more experience and more importantly, they love us 100%. And it may not be the best choice, but it definitely is a considerate and thoughtful decision for us. Like when they try to decide the major for us, it’s very natural we don’t need to fight hard with them, all we need to do is to look at it as a need to pass a narrow training period in college.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: As I mentioned in class, we would encounter ideas that are radically different from our own, they may come from our family members or our friends or people around us. When someone challenges our viewpoints, some would get aggressive and try hard to defend their own ideas, while others are willing to embrace new and novel ideas and incorporate them into their own existing knowledge and understanding of the world if their own ideas are wrong. If you were in a similar situation where someone downplay your ideas, what would be your attitude—hold on firm to your own ideas or be willing to change your mind? Why?

students.name: Ross
students.text: I think if others are persuasive, I would be willing to change my mind. We have to admit that we don’t know everything and the things we do know may not be true. To become a better person, we should be open-minded towards different voices. For example, if scientists hold firm to their own beliefs, we would be living with all kinds of false hypotheses.

students.name: Jack
students.text: I would probably fight back if others challenge my guiding principles or core values. Because it feels devastated to have our beliefs demolished by others. We would lose our confidence or even our identity if we just give in or compromise. Also, there are weasels and losers out there that are trying to pull us down by attacking our deepest-held beliefs. They make themselves feel better this way.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Professor James: In recent years, many children would rather stay at home playing video games than go out for activities during their leisure time. Some parents are concerned that too much exposure to electronic products may be detrimental to children's growth. In response to this phenomenon, should children be forced to participate in various outdoor activities (such as sports, gymnastics, clubs, etc.), even if they would rather sit at home and play video games all day?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think it is a wise move for parents to force their children to get involved in extracurricular activities. Compared with being sedentary all day long, more outdoor time is linked with improved motor development and lower obesity rates and myopia risk. What’s more, safely getting some sun also helps children make vitamin D that their bodies need to stay healthy and strong.

students.name: Ben
students.text: I don't think children should be forced to do so. Some children can enjoy themselves when they are involved in a soccer team or a club, but others may not. For example, if a child has not yet adapted to the community or peer clubs, he or she will think that participating in activities with unfamiliar people is very challenging and stressful. Therefore, it is best for them to stay at home and play games to spend their rest days in a relaxing way.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Dr. Bruce With the development of social media and electronic devices, people can get access to news whenever and wherever they are. However, among all the new reports, most of them may have nothing to do with people’s daily life. Do you think people should know about events happening around the world even if they have little influence on their daily lives?

students.name: Lysol
students.text: I think it is important for one to learn from others' experience, so that we can prepare ourselves if one day the same thing happens to us. Besides, as the globalization starts to bloom, it is fair to say that one can make influence throughout the world, let along a single event. For example, the increasing price of wheat in England may in the end affects the cereal price in Taiwan.

students.name: Kathy
students.text: I don't think people need to know about news that has little impact on their lives. First, this is a waste of time. The news content is always illustrated with pictures or videos, attracting readers to read, which takes a lot of time. These hours could have been used for work, study, or exercise. Moreover, people cannot determine whether the information is true or not, thus being misled by fake news.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Over the next few weeks, we are going to learn about the diverse culture of different countries all over the world. Of course, the culture diversity imposes tremendous effects on the development of human society. Before the next class, here is a question for the class discussion board: what do you think is the most significant effect of learning about another culture?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think that the main effect that learning about another culture has is to help us have a better understanding of another country’s history, tradition and development, which is beneficial for our academic learning. When dealing with academic issues, the in-depth understanding of another culture can help us be more insightful.

students.name: Paul
students.text: In my opinion, knowing about another culture is an effective way to broaden our horizons and expand our knowledge in different fields. Last week I read a book about American history, I learned about some significant historical events and figures in this nation, which was interesting and fascinating.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: We are all exposed to diverse culture especially with the widespread of internet. Of course, there are a variety of ways to get a better understanding of another culture. Before the next class, here is a question for the class discussion board: what do you think is the most effective way to learn about another culture?

students.name: Claire
students.text: In my opinion, traveling to a country with a different cultural background is an effective way to expand our knowledge in this culture. Having a trip to another country is a direct way to have a greater exposure to a new culture, since we will have the opportunity to interact with local people coming from another cultural background.

students.name: Paul
students.text: I think that reading books in another language can help us have a better understanding of another culture. Last week I read a book about Japanese culture, I learned about some significant historical events and figures in its different dynasties, which helped me get an in-depth understanding of Japanese culture.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Many of the goods and services we depend on daily are now supplied by intelligent, automated machines rather than human beings. For example, robots build cars and other goods on assembly lines, where once there were human workers. Many of our phone conversations are now conducted not with people but with sophisticated technologies. Intelligent machines are generally seen as a sign of progress, but what is lost when we replace humans with machines? Do you think the accelerating variety and prevalence of intelligent machines have the meaning of their presence in our lives?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think automation has deprived us of many things in our life. Here I want to mention what we lose with the replacement of people by machines is some part of our own humanity. Even our mundane daily encounters no longer require from us basic courtesy, respect, and tolerance for other people. It is awful!

students.name: Paul
students.text: I don't see any disadvantages of intelligent, automated machines. Machines are good at low-skill, repetitive jobs, and at high-speed, extremely precise jobs. In both cases they work better than humans. This efficiency leads to a more prosperous and progressive world for everyone.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: People today are almost never free from their electronic devices. They spend plenty of time on their phone, tablets, and computers. But a growing number of people are beginning to question this practice. They believe that an overdependence on electronic devices is harmful to their health, their relationships, and perhaps their humanity. Accordingly, they adopt and recommend deliberate “tech-free time” during which they avoid the use of the technologies so prevalent in the world around them. Given the predominance of digital technologies in our daily lives, is it worth adopting some self-imposed tech-free time?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I don't believe tech-free time is valuable in our daily life. Actually, technology has become part of our life, and no one can live without it. Being connected to technology is what defines us as modern people. There’s no point in pretending that we do not depend on and enjoy our digital world.

students.name: Paul
students.text: For me, some tech free time is essential for restoring us to our true selves. Our devices keep us from experiencing and appreciating the real world around. We need to put away our digital devices and embrace our true life. Constantly being connected to technology can cause stress and anxiety. Taking a break from technology can help us relax and reduce stress.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Early exposure to technology is becoming increasingly common in today's society, with many children having access to electronic devices such as smartphones, tablets, and computers at a young age. While technology can provide children with a vast array of educational resources, facilitate social interactions with friends and family members, spending too much time in front of screens can lead to certain health problems. What’s your opinion of children’s use of technology in a young age? Do the benefits of early exposure to technology outweigh the disadvantages?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I don't see many benefits of children's exposure to technology. I think for kids, the less time spent with screens, the better. Decades of studies have proved that watching too much TV can lead to obesity, violence and attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder. I have also read an article showing that social media—specifically the sight of others looking happy in photos—can make kids feel depressed and insecure.

students.name: Paul
students.text: I think the main merit technology brought for kids is to broaden their horizons. Social-networking sites offer unprecedented learning opportunities for children. Online, kids can engage with specialized communities of interest, and they’re no longer limited by what’s offered in school. I am learning guitar by following a teacher on Tik-tok, and it is fascinating!

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Communication technologies like cell phone, e-mail, text message and online social networks have improved the quality of human relationship. Meanwhile, it may also bring some problems to the society. What do you think is the most significant effect that communication technologies have on people? Why do you think communication technologies has this effect?

students.name: Claire
students.text: Communication technologies help people keep in touch with friends and family more easily and frequently. These technologies enables people write or talk to their family and friends through distance, even face-to-face. By chatting with my friends and family on FaceTime, not only can I hear their voices, but also I can see their facial expressions and body movements. I think Internet brings people closer than ever, and it makes communication very instant and effective.

students.name: Paul
students.text: I think social media may insulate us from our friends and family. Although social platforms make communication easier and more flexible, they can have negative impact on our relationships with the people who we live with. According to our experience, it is not unusual to witness that people constantly look at their smartphones or tablets rather than talking to each other in a family or friends’ reunion. Consequently, sometimes social networks might harm our relationships with our loved ones.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Mobile phones allow people to stay connected with friends, family, and coworkers while on the go. People can watch videos, play games, or listen to music to pass the time. It also provides a way for people to call for help in emergency situations. However, some people have complained about the noise and disturbance resulted from the use of mobile phones in public. Should mobile phones not be used in public places such as buses, trains, restaurants, and cinemas?

students.name: Claire
students.text: While in public, people need to be more aware of their situations so that they will not hurt themselves. By using their phones on buses or subways, people may sometimes put themselves in danger. Just the other day I saw a man have an accident while getting off the bus. He was busy talking on his phone when he slipped and fell on the ground. I heard that he broke his leg. I am sure that he now regrets using his cell phone while taking public transportation.

students.name: Paul
students.text: I think phones should not be prohibited in public places. Cell phones have become integral tools for most businesspeople. Since many businesspeople are often out of the office, they need to be able to get in touch with people wherever they are. My uncle is a salesman, and he has to answer his phone every time it rings. If he could not answer his phone on public transportation, which he takes a lot, he would lose a lot of business opportunities. No one has the right to prevent another person from making a living.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: In our last class, we talked about famous historical sites and artifacts around the world. Now I want you to think about one thing and have a discussion: Do you think the government should spend money on preserving all the old buildings and sites rather than building new constructions? Why?

students.name: Hailey
students.text: I totally agree with this statement. Old buildings and sites are a reflection of a country’s history. So, it makes perfect sense to spend money on preserving them. Think about this. When we are all old and our grandchildren ask about our history, wouldn’t it be better to take them to the sites than just showing them pictures?

students.name: Cameron
students.text: To be honest, I don’t think it’s necessary to spend money on preserving old buildings. We are currently in such a fast changing world where histories and miracles are being made every second, which makes it our priority to make economic and technological breakthroughs. Anything would soon become old and abandoned in a world like this. So, there’s no need to spend money preserving old buildings.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: As I was saying, some universities don’t have classes where students can learn about world history. However, some universities require students to take a course on world history in order to graduate. I want you to have a discussion about this and tell me do you think all students should learn about world history at university.

students.name: Steve
students.text: I believe all students should learn about world history at university. Through learning, we can have a better understanding of the heritage of humankind, and thus having a clear mind about where we came from and where we are heading. In my opinion, this benefit is universal.

students.name: Monica
students.text: I don’t think all students should learn about world history at university. Some students like myself just aren’t that interested in culture and history. We prefer to spend our time on doing science experiments and field research. So, if it becomes a compulsory thing, it’s likely to be a waste of time for us.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: As we were discussing last week, people are now communicating and engaging in activities with people from different countries a lot more than before. We now have access to so many things from other countries, like English movies, Japanese cuisine and so on. Some people believe that introduction of foreign culture undermines their local culture. I want to hear about your take on this.

students.name: Will
students.text: I agree with this statement without any hesitation. From what I have observed, the introduction of foreign culture is causing people’s loss of interest in their own culture, especially among youngsters. They would spend time and money celebrating foreign festivals, but they barely remember the tradition of their own. Therefore, I think this statement sounds true enough.

students.name: Nancy
students.text: I would say it sounds too drastic. It is true that different cultures are blending in with each other right now. But I believe it’s actually a good thing for all. Every country has its merits when it comes to customs and traditions, which can probably be a great asset for other countries to learn from. So, I think we should all welcome the introduction of foreign culture.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: In a world full of changes and possibilities, we face challenges and problems all the time. And people are trying so hard to find solutions to various problems. Many hold that it is important for a country to learn from the past in order to solve the problems of the present and even the future. How do you feel about this statement?And why?

students.name: Ronald
students.text: I think it is absolutely true that we should learn from the past. The past is like a mirror that reflects the mistakes that our ancestors had made and the lessons that we as descendants can learn from. Therefore, in my point of view, we should definitely learn from the past and then deal with present problems.

students.name: Leah
students.text: Personally, I don’t think it’s wise to linger over the past. If we plan to solve problems of the present and the future, we should stick to more practical solutions which can be drawn from our understanding and interpretation of the problems themselves. Past history might be outdated. So, I think it is best to find avenues out of our current situations instead of past ones.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Last week, we talked about cultural heritage. As you can see, right now it seems that we have easier access to various resources through the internet. You can easily watch and appreciate different cultural and historical sites and artifacts just on a screen. But do you think the internet has a positive influence on appreciating and preserving cultural heritage? Please have a discussion about this.

students.name: Ryan
students.text: I think the internet has a negative influence on appreciating and preserving cultural heritage. In the past, if you wanted to know something about Mona Lisa, you’d have to spend time doing your research, going to the library or asking your professor for help. But now, people just click buttons and then forget about it in three days. So, I don’t think it’s a good thing.

students.name: Phoebe
students.text: Internet definitely has a positive influence on people’s appreciating cultural heritage. It provides the most convenient way for people to look at a famous painting or a sculpture without even getting out of their house, which was not imaginable a hundred years ago.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we're going to talk about whether it's better to keep a record of your own experiences by updating pictures and information on social networking sites or not. Some people believe that sharing their experiences and activities on social media is a great way to keep a record of their lives, while others prefer to keep their experiences private. Which do you prefer, and why?

students.name: David
students.text: I prefer to keep my experiences private. I believe that personal experiences are something to be enjoyed in the moment and shared only with the people who are close to me. Additionally, I think that sharing too much personal information online can lead to privacy and security concerns. It's important to maintain some level of privacy and control over what information is available about oneself online.

students.name: Karen
students.text: I disagree with David; I think sharing experiences online is a great way to stay connected with friends and family who might not live close by. Not only that, but social media provides an easy way to track and reminisce about our experiences. Some people might say that sharing online indicates a lack of intimacy, but I would argue that anyone who's truly close to us will appreciate the opportunity to keep up with our lives.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we're going to discuss whether it's always a good idea for people to take a short breakaway from news as a result of the fast development of society and constantly reported news stories. Some people may argue that taking a break from the news can help to reduce stress and anxiety, while others may say that it's important to stay informed about what's happening in the world. Which do you agree with, and why?

students.name: Jared
students.text: I believe that it's always a good idea for people to take a break from the news. We are bombarded with news stories constantly, and it can sometimes be overwhelming or distressing. By taking a break, we can reduce our stress and anxiety levels and even come back to the news with a fresh perspective. Additionally, consuming too much news can cause us to become desensitized to important events, so taking a break can help us stay engaged and invested in the news when we come back to it.

students.name: Emily
students.text: I disagree with Jared; I think staying informed about current events is extremely important. There are so many things happening in the world that affect our daily lives, and staying informed can help us make more informed decisions about our own lives. Additionally, taking a break from the news could cause us to miss out on important events or decisions that could have long-lasting impacts. While I understand the desire to reduce stress levels, I think it's crucial to continue staying informed about the world around us.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today we’re going to talk about how most advertisements make products seem much better than they really are. Do you think this is an ethical practice? Why or why not?

students.name: John
students.text: I believe that most advertisements are misleading and unethical. They exaggerate the qualities of a product to attract customers, which creates false expectations and often results in disappointment. This can lead to a lack of trust between consumers and businesses. It's important for companies to be transparent and honest in their marketing campaigns, and to provide accurate information about their products.

students.name: Samantha
students.text: While I understand John's point, I think that advertisements are a necessary part of the marketplace. Companies need to promote their products to compete in the market, and they use advertising to do so. However, I do think that there should be regulations in place to prevent companies from making false or exaggerated claims. It's important for consumers to be able to make informed decisions about the products they buy, and for businesses to respect the integrity of the buying process.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today we’re going to talk about the impact of social media on society. Some people believe that social media has positive effects by bringing people closer together and facilitating communication. Others argue that social media has negative effects on mental health and social interaction. What is your opinion on the issue?

students.name: Mia
students.text: I believe that social media has a mostly positive impact on society. Social media connects people who may not otherwise be able to communicate, and it allows individuals to express themselves and share their experiences with others. Social media has also become an important tool for social activism and raising awareness about important issues. While there may be some negative effects on mental health and social interaction, these can be mitigated through responsible use of social media.

students.name: Jake
students.text: I disagree with Mia's view. Social media has mostly negative effects on society. Social media has been linked to negative mental health outcomes such as depression, anxiety, and decreased self-esteem. It also fosters a culture of comparison and competition, leading to feelings of inadequacy and dissatisfaction. Social media can also be a source of disinformation and fake news, which can have detrimental effects on individuals and society as a whole.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today we’re going to talk about the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) on society. Some people believe that AI has positive effects by improving efficiency and productivity in various industries. Others argue that AI has negative effects by displacing human workers and leading to job loss. What is your opinion on the issue?

students.name: David
students.text: I believe that AI has a mostly positive impact on society. AI can improve efficiency and productivity in industries such as healthcare, transportation, and manufacturing. AI can also be used to address issues such as climate change, disease prevention, and disaster response. While there may be some negative effects on job displacement, AI has the potential to create new and innovative jobs that we haven't even thought of yet.

students.name: Sarah
students.text: I disagree with David's view. AI has mostly negative effects on society. AI is already leading to job displacement in many industries, and it could lead to mass unemployment in the future. AI also raises ethical concerns over privacy and security, as well as the possibility of AI achieving intelligence that surpasses that of humans and becoming a threat to our well-being. While AI has some benefits, the negative effects outweigh the positive.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: For this week’s discussion, let’s think about how playing video games affects children’s development. Some parents concern that video games have tremendous negative effects on children’s learning while some people experts claim that video games are beneficial for children’s development. Before the next class, here is a question for the class discussion board: do you think video games have more positive or negative effects on children?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think that playing video games is harmful for children’s development. As we all know, a lot of children are addicted to playing computer and cellphone games because they lack of the self-control ability, which hinders their learning. Also, children will be lazier and unwilling to do exercise in free time.

students.name: Paul
students.text: In my opinion, playing video games is beneficial for children since it is an effective way for them to release relax. It is common that most children are under great pressure due to the busy schedule, so playing video games is significant for them to release the stress and refresh themselves.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we're going to discuss the most important factor to help increase productivity. There are three factors to consider: having an environment that is free of noise and distractions, knowing that you will receive a reward, and doing something you are interested in. Which factor do you think is the most important? Why?

students.name: Susan
students.text: I believe that doing something we're interested in is the most important factor when it comes to productivity. When we're interested in what we're doing, we're more likely to stay focused and engaged over longer periods of time. This increased engagement and satisfaction with the task leads to higher levels of productivity. While an environment free of distractions and a guaranteed reward can be motivational factors, they aren't enough to make up for the lack of personal interest in the task at hand.

students.name: Mark
students.text: As someone who has worked in a distracting environment before, I believe that having an environment that's free of noise and distractions is the most important factor for productivity. When we're constantly interrupted or distracted, our focus and motivation suffer, which can lead to lower productivity levels. Additionally, an environment free of distractions helps us stay on task and accomplish our goals more efficiently. While being interested in a task and a guaranteed reward for a job well done can also motivate us, neither is as essential as having a productive environment.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we're going to talk about the best way to find a job in a new city. There are three main methods that are commonly used: asking friends for job leads, searching for job ads online or in print, and sending a letter of introduction or resume to a company you're interested in. Which method do you think is the most effective and why?

students.name: Sara
students.text: In my experience, the best way to find a job in a new city is by reaching out to your network of friends, family, and acquaintances. By asking for job leads, you can tap into the hidden job market and find opportunities that may not be advertised publicly. Additionally, having a personal connection can help you stand out in the job search process and give you an edge over other candidates.

students.name: David
students.text: I disagree. I think the most effective way to find a job in a new city is to search for job ads online or in print. Job ads are posted by companies that are actively seeking to fill positions, so you know that there is an actual job opening to apply to. Additionally, many job search websites allow you to filter your search by location, skills, and other factors to narrow down your options. While friends and personal connections can certainly be helpful, job ads provide a more direct route to a potential job opportunity.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today we’re going to talk about work-life balance and job satisfaction. Some people believe that employees should prioritize work above all else to be successful, while others argue that a healthy work-life balance is essential for job satisfaction and overall well-being. What is your opinion on the issue?

students.name: Emily
students.text: I believe that a healthy work-life balance is essential for job satisfaction and overall well-being. When employees are able to prioritize their personal lives and relationships, they are more likely to be happy and fulfilled. In addition, a positive work-life balance can reduce stress and burnout, leading to higher productivity and better job performance.

students.name: David
students.text: I disagree with Emily's view. Employees should prioritize work above all else to be successful. Success requires hard work, dedication, and a willingness to make sacrifices. Those who prioritize their personal lives and relationships over work are less likely to achieve their goals and reach their full potential.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today we’re going to talk about a shortened workweek. Some people believe that a shortened workweek can improve work-life balance, job satisfaction, and productivity, while others argue that it may have negative impacts on businesses and the economy. What is your opinion on the issue?

students.name: Sarah
students.text: I believe that a shortened workweek can improve work-life balance, job satisfaction, and productivity. By reducing the number of hours worked per week, employees have more time to focus on their personal lives and responsibilities, leading to less stress and burnout. Additionally, the resulting increase in job satisfaction and motivation can lead to higher productivity and better job performance.

students.name: John
students.text: I disagree with Sarah's view. A shortened workweek may have negative impacts on businesses and the economy. Fewer hours worked means less output, which can lead to lower profits and economic growth. Additionally, businesses may not be able to afford to pay employees for the same amount of work done in a shorter time period, leading to decreased wages and layoffs.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today we’re going to talk about job-sharing. Some people believe that job-sharing can improve work-life balance and job satisfaction, while others argue that it may have negative impacts on businesses and the economy. What is your opinion on the issue?

students.name: Karen
students.text: I believe that job-sharing can improve work-life balance and job satisfaction. By sharing a job with another person, employees can reduce the number of hours worked per week, allowing for more time to focus on personal responsibilities and interests. Additionally, job-sharing can increase flexibility, reduce stress and burnout, and improve overall well-being.

students.name: Tom
students.text: I disagree with Karen's view. Job-sharing may have negative impacts on businesses and the economy. It can increase costs related to training and management, as well as reduce productivity and efficiency. Additionally, job-sharing may lead to difficulties in communication and collaboration between employees, leading to decreased job performance and lower business profits.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we're going to talk about one challenge of economic development: it seems difficult the keep the balance between economic development and environment conservation. In the past decades, in order to pursue economic prosperity, the natural environment is severely polluted and damaged. Then comes the idea that we should lay more emphasis on environment preservation though this may slow down the economic development. So, the question for the discussion board is: should economic development be slowed down considering environment conservation?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think environment is the future of humans. Without the environment, human society cannot develop any more. For example, if we cut down all trees, how can we develop industry related to logging, paper making and furniture production. There is no end of economic development, but sacrificing environment is never a good idea.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Although environment protection is important, economic growth is more critical for us. In some developing countries, the primary goal is still developing the economy and eliminating the poverty, which means that it is not a wise move to slow down the economy because of the concerns about environment protection.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: There are all kinds of workers: office workers, physical laborers, service providers, etc. However, the number of bosses is significant smaller compared to that of workers. It is common sense that bosses tend to earn much more than common workers, so much so that it seems unjust because bosses occupy a large chunk of the profits procured by their businesses while seemingly doing much less work than their employees. Most workers only drool over the enormous profits of their bosses, but they rarely take actions to start their own businesses. That is really because it is very hard to succeed in the business world. What do you think is an important factor that makes a successful entrepreneur?

students.name: Claire
students.text: Capital. If a startup business is to survive and thrive, a sustainable cash flow is necessary. Virtually all aspects of running a business cost money: manpower, venue, technology development, production, and marketing. If a business runs out of investment while not making profits, it is doomed.

students.name: Paul
students.text: I think a successful boss should possess strong management skills, especially manpower management. A boss might be highly talented and committed to putting ideas into action, but eventually, he/she will need help from a team to run his/her business. Strong manpower management skills help bosses to identify and keep good employees, put their unique merits into use, promote those who have made the biggest contribution to the company, and, of course, fire incompetent or maleficent workers. Such goals might seem trivial when a business is still small, but when it grows, employee management will become one of the top priorities of bosses.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Globalization has changed our world dramatically in the past decades. As the core of globalization, international trades have involved virtually all countries. As people enjoy products from all over the world, they also become generally better off thanks to larger markets and more work opportunities. However, opinions vary greatly regarding the benefits and losses of developing countries in international trades. Typically, developing countries sell cheap raw materials and low-level products while purchasing expensive hi-tech products or luxuries from developed countries. Some point out that this is actually an exploitation of poor countries by the rich ones. What is your opinion, do developing countries benefit from or get ripped off during international trades?

students.name: Claire
students.text: If one sees the pictures of sweatshops in developing countries, he/she will know immediately that developing countries are actually suffering losses in international trades. Developing countries normally lack high technology, and even if they develop their own technology, most of the international markets have been seized by early companies from developed countries. As a result, they have no choice but to sell their raw materials, if any, or labor-intensive products manufactured by their cheap laborers. To be fair, the lavish lives of those tycoons in rich countries are built on their exploitation of the poor workers in developing countries.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Much as I sympathize the poor workers in developing countries, I have to point out a plain fact: those workers would have been poorer were it not for their "sweatshops". Countries are like people: they need to cooperate and compete with each other through international trades. During such cooperation, poor countries can get their due shares of the wealth thus generated, which might not be much because they have not much to offer. But this gain is already much bigger than if they were to close their borders and rely on agriculture and a tiny number of industries. If there are no such sweatshops producing products for an international market, workers will have nowhere to earn their wages. I have another class to attend, but I will elaborate on the competition part later.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Dr. Lopez For the past few decades, people have shown a great interest towards unhealthy cousins and soft drinks. It is said that local shops are highly responsible for this trend and thus, Some individuals think that these outlets should not be permitted to freely sell such scientifically proven detrimental things, whereas others are against. What is your opinion?

students.name: Andy
students.text: I believe unhealthy food and beverages should not be sold. The reason is that people are suffering from lots of diseases by eating them. For example, the rate of people developing heart disease, obesity and diabetes has been increasing alarmingly. Many researchers point out that causes of death of human beings are junks items. Besides, many shops try to sell cheap unhealthy food just to make profits. Thus, the government should impose strict rules on selling unhealthy products.

students.name: Jane
students.text: In today's fast-paced life where both the parents are working, these foods come as a handy option. Although they are not good for health, a small amount doesn't affect the body much. What's more, some big brands like Mc Donald's and Pizza Hut have many outlets across the globe, which helps the government in dealing with unemployment by creating jobs and contribute to the country's economy and GDP. Thus, stopping them to sell products can affect the economy.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today we’re going to talk about public and private transportation. Some people believe that public transportation is essential for reducing traffic congestion and promoting sustainable transportation options. Others argue that private transportation is necessary for individual freedom and convenience. What is your opinion on the issue?

students.name: Lisa
students.text: I believe that public transportation is essential for reducing traffic congestion and promoting sustainable transportation options. Public transportation provides an affordable and accessible option for individuals to get around, particularly in urban areas where traffic congestion is a major problem. Public transportation also reduces pollution and greenhouse gas emissions, improves air quality, and promotes a more sustainable way of living.

students.name: Tom
students.text: I disagree with Lisa's view. Private transportation is necessary for individual freedom and convenience. Private transportation allows individuals to travel on their own schedules, at their own pace, without the inconvenience of waiting for public transportation. Additionally, some individuals may have unique travel needs or work in industries that require private transportation, such as construction or delivery.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today we’re going to talk about how to use and develop a large building available. Some people believe that large buildings should be repurposed for new uses that benefit the community, while others argue that large buildings should be demolished and replaced with new construction. What is your opinion on the issue?

students.name: Emily
students.text: I believe that large buildings should be repurposed for new uses that benefit the community. Repurposing large buildings can be an effective way to revitalize neighborhoods and provide new opportunities for businesses, cultural institutions, and public services. In addition, repurposing large buildings can help to preserve historic architecture and reduce waste associated with demolition and construction.

students.name: David
students.text: I disagree with Emily's view. Large buildings that are no longer functional or outdated should be demolished and replaced with new construction. New construction can provide more efficient and sustainable buildings that meet modern standards for safety, accessibility, and energy efficiency. Additionally, demolition and new construction can create new jobs and stimulate economic growth.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today we’re going to talk about governmental support for a certain group of people. Some people believe that the government should provide support for older people who cannot work, such as those over 70 years old. Others argue that the government should provide support for workers who have recently lost their jobs. What is your opinion on the issue?

students.name: Sarah
students.text: I believe that the government should provide support for older people who cannot work, such as those over 70 years old. Older people may face health problems that prevent them from working, and they may not have access to retirement savings or pensions. The government has a responsibility to provide support for those who cannot work, including financial assistance, access to healthcare, and other services.

students.name: John
students.text: I disagree with Sarah's view. The government should provide support for workers who have recently lost their jobs. Losing a job can be a traumatic experience, and workers may need financial assistance, training, and resources to find new employment. Supporting workers who have recently lost their jobs can help to mitigate the negative effects of unemployment on individuals and families, and promote economic growth.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll be discussing the issue of companies that provide important products and services, but also cause environmental damage. Some people argue that the government should impose more penalties on these companies, such as higher taxes and larger fines, to prevent them from causing further environmental damage. Do you agree with this approach, or do you believe that there are other ways to address this issue? Why?

students.name: Sarah
students.text: I strongly believe that the government should impose stricter penalties on companies that cause environmental damage. While it's true that these companies provide important goods and services, they also have a responsibility to minimize their environmental impact. If these companies are allowed to continue damaging the environment without consequence, the long-term ecological consequences could be disastrous. Higher taxes and larger fines are an effective way to hold companies accountable for their actions and encourage them to invest in sustainable practices.

students.name: John
